enrichment.short_description: UptimeRobot is a cloud-based website and API monitoring service that checks uptime, SSL certificates, and response times from multiple global locations and alerts teams the moment something goes down.
enrichment.seo_title: UptimeRobot Review: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what UptimeRobot costs, how its uptime and SSL monitoring works, and whether its free plan and paid tiers fit your team.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: UptimeRobot is a website, API, and infrastructure monitoring service founded in 2010 by Daniel Rimille and Umut Muhaddisoglu. In November 2019 it was acquired by Pale Fire Capital, an investment firm that also owns other bootstrapped SaaS products; the company now operates out of Sliema, Malta. UptimeRobot checks HTTP/HTTPS endpoints, keywords, ping, ports, cron jobs, DNS records, and SSL/domain expiration from multiple global monitoring locations, alerting teams via email, SMS, voice call, mobile push, and integrations like Slack, Microsoft Teams, Discord, and PagerDuty when something goes down or degrades. It also offers customizable public status pages and lightweight incident management with notes and timelines. The product uses a freemium model: the Free plan supports 50 monitors at 5-minute check intervals with 3 months of data retention; paid tiers (Solo, Team, and Enterprise) reduce the check interval to 60 seconds (30 seconds on Enterprise), add more monitors, longer data retention, more status pages, and team seats, roughly starting around 10 euro per month and scaling to enterprise pricing with SOC 2, CCPA, and GDPR compliance. UptimeRobot reports over 3.3 million registered users and monitors millions of endpoints for customers including WordPress VIP, GitHub, Slack, Intel, and the BBC. Its main points of comparison are Uptime Kuma, a free self-hosted alternative with a similar interface, and heavier full-stack observability platforms like Better Uptime, Pingdom, and StatusCake, against which UptimeRobot is generally positioned as the lower-cost, easier-to-set-up option for basic uptime and SSL monitoring rather than deep application performance monitoring.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is UptimeRobot
paragraphs: UptimeRobot is a cloud-based monitoring service that continuously checks whether websites, APIs, servers, and other endpoints are online and performing correctly. Founded in 2010 and now owned by Pale Fire Capital, it has grown into one of the most widely used uptime monitoring tools, watching millions of monitors for individual developers, agencies, and larger engineering teams.

The service works by pinging a target URL, port, or keyword from multiple locations around the world on a set interval, then firing alerts the moment a check fails so a false positive from a single region does not trigger unnecessary noise. Beyond basic up/down checks, it also tracks SSL certificate and domain expiration dates, response times, and DNS record changes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: UptimeRobot supports HTTP/HTTPS, ping, port, keyword, cron job, DNS, SSL, and UDP monitoring, with multi-location checks to reduce false alarms. Alerting covers email, SMS, voice calls, mobile push notifications, and more than a dozen integrations such as Slack, Microsoft Teams, Discord, and PagerDuty.

Customers can build branded, password-protectable public status pages to communicate incidents to their own users, and collaborate on incidents internally with notes and timelines. Maintenance windows let teams silence alerts during planned downtime, and role-based access controls govern who on a team can view or edit monitors.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: UptimeRobot's Free plan includes 50 monitors checked every 5 minutes with 3 months of data retention, making it usable for personal projects and small sites without a credit card. Paid plans, Solo, Team, and Enterprise, drop the check interval to 60 seconds (30 seconds on Enterprise), add more monitors, longer retention, more status pages, and team seats, starting at roughly 10 euro per month and scaling up based on monitor count and features like SOC 2 and GDPR compliance on Enterprise.
SMS and voice alert credits are sold separately as one-time top-ups, and all paid subscriptions come with a 14-day money-back guarantee.

features.title: Multi-type monitoring
features.description: Monitors HTTP/HTTPS, ping, port, keyword, cron job, DNS, SSL certificate, domain expiration, and UDP endpoints.

features.title: Multi-location checks
features.description: Verifies downtime from several global regions before alerting, cutting down on false positives.

features.title: Multi-channel alerting
features.description: Sends alerts via email, SMS, voice call, push notification, and integrations like Slack, Teams, Discord, and PagerDuty.

features.title: Public status pages
features.description: Branded, password-protectable status pages that display live and historical uptime to end users.

features.title: Incident management
features.description: Lets teams log notes, track incident timelines, and coordinate responses from within the platform.

features.title: Maintenance windows
features.description: Suppresses alerts during scheduled downtime so planned maintenance doesn't trigger false alarms.

features.title: Team access controls
features.description: Role-based login and notify seats let organizations control who can view, edit, or receive alerts.

features.title: SSL and domain monitoring
features.description: Tracks SSL certificate validity and domain expiration dates to prevent surprise outages.

pros: Generous free plan with 50 monitors, no credit card required
Very quick setup with a simple, no-frills interface
Wide range of monitor types beyond basic HTTP checks
Affordable entry-level paid pricing compared to enterprise APM tools
Large integration library for alerting and status pages
cons: Free plan is limited to 5-minute check intervals, too slow for latency-sensitive services
Deeper analytics and longer data retention require higher-tier paid plans
Not a full observability or APM platform like Datadog or New Relic
SMS/voice alerts require separately purchased credits
Some advanced features like custom SSO are enterprise-only

faqs.question: Is UptimeRobot free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, UptimeRobot offers a permanent free plan supporting up to 50 monitors with 5-minute check intervals and no credit card required.

faqs.question: How often does UptimeRobot check my site on the free plan?
faqs.answer: Free plan monitors are checked every 5 minutes; paid plans reduce this to 60 seconds, and Enterprise plans check every 30 seconds.

faqs.question: Who owns UptimeRobot?
faqs.answer: UptimeRobot was founded in 2010 by Daniel Rimille and Umut Muhaddisoglu and has been owned by the investment firm Pale Fire Capital since 2019.

faqs.question: Does UptimeRobot monitor SSL certificates?
faqs.answer: Yes, it tracks SSL certificate validity and domain expiration and can alert before either lapses.

faqs.question: Can I create a public status page with UptimeRobot?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid and free plans both support customizable status pages, with more pages and branding options on higher tiers.

faqs.question: How is UptimeRobot different from Uptime Kuma?
faqs.answer: UptimeRobot is a hosted commercial SaaS service, while Uptime Kuma is a free, open-source uptime monitor you install and host yourself.

faqs.question: What alert channels does UptimeRobot support?
faqs.answer: Email, SMS, voice calls, mobile push notifications, and integrations such as Slack, Microsoft Teams, Discord, and PagerDuty.

faqs.question: Does UptimeRobot offer a money-back guarantee?
faqs.answer: Yes, new paid subscriptions come with a 14-day money-back guarantee.

use_cases.title: Solo developers monitoring personal projects
use_cases.description: Indie developers use the free plan to watch small sites, side projects, and APIs without paying anything.

use_cases.title: Agencies monitoring client websites
use_cases.description: Digital agencies use Team and Enterprise plans with branded status pages to monitor and report on many client sites at once.

use_cases.title: SaaS teams tracking API and infrastructure uptime
use_cases.description: Engineering teams monitor production APIs, DNS records, and SSL certificates to catch outages before customers do.

enrichment.short_description: Uptrace is an open-source, OpenTelemetry-native observability platform built on ClickHouse that unifies distributed traces, metrics, and logs in a single dashboard for application performance monitoring.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (self-hosted or 50GB/month free on Cloud, then from $0.075/GB)
enrichment.seo_title: Uptrace Review: OpenTelemetry APM Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Uptrace unifies traces, metrics, and logs on OpenTelemetry and ClickHouse. See its pricing, features, and how it compares to Datadog and Grafana.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Uptrace is an open-source application performance monitoring and observability platform founded in 2021 by Vladimir Mihailenco, based in Chisinau, Moldova. It is built entirely on open standards: OpenTelemetry for instrumentation and data collection, and ClickHouse as the underlying columnar database, which lets it handle high-cardinality trace, metric, and log data efficiently. Uptrace unifies distributed tracing, metrics, and logs in one interface, with a service graph that shows dependencies and RED metrics (rate, errors, duration) to help teams spot bottlenecks. It also ingests data from Prometheus, Vector, FluentBit, and CloudWatch, and offers an MCP-compatible interface so AI assistants like Claude can query telemetry data in plain language. The Community Edition is fully open source under AGPL-3.0 and free to self-host with no feature limits via Docker, Kubernetes, or Ansible. Uptrace Cloud uses usage-based pricing: the first 50GB of traces/logs and 5,000 metric timeseries per month are free, after which data is billed per gigabyte (starting around $0.075-$0.10/GB with volume discounts) and metrics per million datapoints, with unlimited users, services, and hosts at every tier -- no per-seat charges. Uptrace positions itself as a lower-cost, vendor-neutral alternative to Datadog, New Relic, and Splunk, citing cost comparisons where a large engineering team's monthly bill can run roughly 90 percent cheaper than equivalent Datadog or New Relic usage. It competes most directly with other OpenTelemetry-native, ClickHouse-backed tools such as SigNoz and with self-hosted stacks combining Grafana, Tempo, and Loki.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Uptrace
paragraphs: Uptrace is an open-source observability and application performance monitoring platform that brings traces, metrics, and logs together in one dashboard, built specifically around the OpenTelemetry standard so teams avoid vendor lock-in to a proprietary agent.

Founded in 2021 and based in Chisinau, Moldova, Uptrace stores telemetry data in ClickHouse, a fast columnar database, which allows it to handle very high-cardinality attributes and large data volumes at a lower cost than many closed-source APM vendors.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Uptrace provides distributed tracing, metrics, and log correlation in a single unified view, along with a service graph that visualizes dependencies between services and surfaces request rate, error rate, and latency (RED metrics). It ingests data natively from OpenTelemetry SDKs and collectors, plus Prometheus, Vector, FluentBit, and AWS CloudWatch.

An MCP server integration lets AI assistants such as Claude query and summarize telemetry data conversationally. Deployment is flexible, running as a managed cloud service or self-hosted via Docker, Kubernetes, or Ansible, with the self-hosted Community Edition released under the AGPL-3.0 open-source license.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Uptrace Cloud includes 50GB of traces, logs, and metrics plus 5,000 metric timeseries free every month. Beyond that, billing is usage-based: traces and logs are charged per gigabyte ingested (starting around $0.075-$0.10/GB with volume discounts as usage scales) and metrics are billed per million datapoints, with extended retention priced separately per gigabyte-month or timeseries-month.

There are no per-seat, per-host, or per-service fees, and organizations can self-host the Community Edition for free with no feature restrictions, or pay for a licensed on-premises deployment with dedicated support.

features.title: Unified traces, metrics, and logs
features.description: Correlates distributed traces, application metrics, and logs in one dashboard instead of separate tools.

features.title: OpenTelemetry-native
features.description: Built around the OpenTelemetry standard for instrumentation, avoiding proprietary agent lock-in.

features.title: ClickHouse-backed storage
features.description: Uses the ClickHouse columnar database for fast queries over high-cardinality, high-volume telemetry data.

features.title: Service graph and RED metrics
features.description: Visualizes service dependencies and highlights request rate, error rate, and duration bottlenecks.

features.title: Multi-source ingestion
features.description: Accepts data from Prometheus, Vector, FluentBit, and CloudWatch in addition to OpenTelemetry.

features.title: AI/MCP integration
features.description: Exposes an MCP server so AI assistants like Claude can query telemetry data in natural language.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Available as a managed cloud service or self-hosted via Docker, Kubernetes, or Ansible.

features.title: Open-source Community Edition
features.description: AGPL-3.0-licensed self-hosted edition with no feature limits, hosted on GitHub.

pros: Fully open source and self-hostable with no artificial feature caps
Usage-based pricing with no per-seat charges, unlike many APM vendors
Built on open standards (OpenTelemetry, ClickHouse) that avoid lock-in
Significantly cheaper than Datadog or New Relic at scale per public comparisons
Generous free tier for both cloud and self-hosted use
cons: Smaller ecosystem and community than established players like Datadog or Grafana
Self-hosting requires operating and scaling a ClickHouse cluster
Less mature enterprise tooling (SSO, advanced RBAC) than large incumbent APM vendors
Documentation and integrations still maturing relative to bigger observability platforms

faqs.question: Is Uptrace open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Uptrace Community Edition is open source under the AGPL-3.0 license and free to self-host with no feature limits.

faqs.question: What database does Uptrace use?
faqs.answer: Uptrace stores telemetry data in ClickHouse, a columnar database chosen for fast queries over high-cardinality data.

faqs.question: Does Uptrace support OpenTelemetry?
faqs.answer: Yes, Uptrace is built natively around the OpenTelemetry standard for collecting traces, metrics, and logs.

faqs.question: How much does Uptrace Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: Uptrace Cloud includes 50GB of data and 5,000 timeseries free per month, then bills usage-based per gigabyte and per million metric datapoints.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Uptrace for free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition can be self-hosted for free via Docker, Kubernetes, or Ansible with no feature restrictions.

faqs.question: Does Uptrace charge per user seat?
faqs.answer: No, Uptrace includes unlimited users, services, and hosts on every plan.

faqs.question: What is Uptrace an alternative to?
faqs.answer: Uptrace is commonly compared to Datadog, New Relic, Splunk, and Grafana/Tempo/Loki stacks as a lower-cost, open-source observability option.

faqs.question: Where is Uptrace based?
faqs.answer: Uptrace was founded in 2021 and is based in Chisinau, Moldova.

use_cases.title: Replacing expensive APM vendors
use_cases.description: Engineering teams migrate from Datadog or New Relic to cut observability costs while keeping trace, metric, and log visibility.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted observability for compliance
use_cases.description: Organizations with data residency or budget constraints self-host Uptrace to keep telemetry data in their own infrastructure.

use_cases.title: OpenTelemetry-native microservices monitoring
use_cases.description: Teams already instrumented with OpenTelemetry use Uptrace to visualize service dependencies and debug latency across microservices.

features: Deploy via Docker, Kubernetes, or Ansible

enrichment.short_description: Upvote RSS is a free, self-hosted, open-source tool that turns top posts from Reddit, Hacker News, Lemmy, Mbin, and GitHub into filtered, enriched RSS feeds for any feed reader.
enrichment.seo_title: Upvote RSS: Self-Hosted RSS Feeds From Reddit and Hacker News
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Upvote RSS turns Reddit, Hacker News, and Lemmy top posts into filtered, self-hosted RSS feeds with AI summaries. See how it works and how to set it up.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Upvote RSS is a free, open-source, self-hosted PHP tool created by developer John Warne that generates curated RSS feeds from social aggregation sites including Reddit, Hacker News, Lemmy, Lobsters, PieFed, Mbin, and GitHub. Rather than subscribing to a raw, high-volume subreddit or Hacker News firehose, users configure a feed by picking a source community or platform and setting filters such as minimum score thresholds, keyword filters, timeframes, and desired posts-per-day, so the resulting feed only contains the most relevant, highly-upvoted content. The tool goes beyond simple feed generation by embedding post media (videos, image galleries, thumbnails), extracting and cleaning article content with built-in parsers, and optionally generating AI-written summaries of linked articles using a choice of providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, DeepSeek, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible API. The project is hosted on GitHub, distributed as open-source software, and is commonly deployed via Docker or a standard PHP web server, making it a favorite among the self-hosting and privacy-conscious RSS reader community documented on sites like selfh.st and r/selfhosted. There is no commercial pricing: the entire tool is free to download, run, and modify, with users supplying their own hosting and, optionally, their own AI provider API keys for the summarization feature. It fills a niche distinct from mainstream feed readers or dedicated commercial monitoring tools by focusing narrowly on converting noisy social-aggregation upvote streams into a low-volume, high-signal RSS feed a person can comfortably read in their existing feed reader.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Upvote RSS
paragraphs: Upvote RSS is a self-hosted, open-source tool that converts the top-voted posts from Reddit, Hacker News, Lemmy, Mbin, PieFed, Lobsters, and GitHub into personalized RSS feeds. Instead of following a subreddit or forum directly and being overwhelmed by volume, users set score thresholds, keyword filters, and timeframes so only the most relevant posts appear in their feed reader.

Built by developer John Warne and distributed as open-source software on GitHub, the project is aimed at people who prefer a curated, low-noise reading experience inside an existing RSS reader rather than checking multiple social platforms directly.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Feeds can be tuned by score threshold, keyword inclusion or exclusion, time window, and target number of posts per day, giving fine control over feed volume and relevance. Upvote RSS embeds post media such as videos, image galleries, and thumbnails directly into the feed and uses parsers to extract clean article content from linked pages.

An optional AI summarization feature generates short article summaries using a configurable provider, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, DeepSeek, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, letting self-hosters choose a provider that fits their budget or privacy requirements.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Upvote RSS is entirely free and open source; there is no paid tier, subscription, or hosted commercial version. Users run it themselves, typically via Docker or a PHP-capable web server, and only pay for their own hosting and, if they enable AI summaries, their own third-party AI API usage.

features.title: Multi-platform feed generation
features.description: Builds RSS feeds from Reddit, Hacker News, Lemmy, Mbin, PieFed, Lobsters, and GitHub.

features.title: Score and keyword filtering
features.description: Filters posts by minimum score, keywords, and timeframe to reduce noise in the resulting feed.

features.title: Posts-per-day tuning
features.description: Lets users configure the target volume of posts delivered per day for a comfortable reading pace.

features.title: Embedded media
features.description: Includes videos, image galleries, and thumbnails directly inside the generated feed entries.

features.title: Content parsing
features.description: Extracts clean article text and featured images from linked pages using built-in parsers.

features.title: AI article summaries
features.description: Optionally generates AI summaries of linked articles using OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Mistral, DeepSeek, or Ollama.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Runs on the user's own infrastructure via Docker or PHP, keeping data and configuration fully under user control.

features.title: Open-source codebase
features.description: Source is publicly available on GitHub for inspection, modification, and self-improvement by the community.

pros: Completely free and open source with no subscription
Fine-grained filtering keeps feeds low-volume and high-signal
Supports multiple social platforms beyond just Reddit
Optional AI summaries save time reading linked articles
Full control over data since it runs on your own server
cons: Requires self-hosting and basic technical setup, not a hosted SaaS
AI summaries require your own third-party API key and incur their own costs
No official support team, relies on community and GitHub issues
Limited to supported source platforms rather than arbitrary websites

faqs.question: Is Upvote RSS free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Upvote RSS is completely free and open source; you only pay for your own hosting and any optional AI API usage.

faqs.question: What platforms can Upvote RSS pull posts from?
faqs.answer: It supports Reddit, Hacker News, Lemmy, Mbin, PieFed, Lobsters, and GitHub.

faqs.question: Do I need to self-host Upvote RSS?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is a self-hosted tool typically deployed via Docker or a PHP-capable web server; there is no official hosted version.

faqs.question: Can Upvote RSS summarize articles with AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can generate AI summaries of linked articles using providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Mistral, DeepSeek, or Ollama, using your own API key.

faqs.question: Who created Upvote RSS?
faqs.answer: Upvote RSS was created by developer John Warne and is maintained as an open-source project on GitHub.

faqs.question: How do I filter which posts appear in my feed?
faqs.answer: You can set a minimum score threshold, keyword filters, a timeframe, and a target number of posts per day when building a feed.

faqs.question: Does Upvote RSS work with any RSS reader?
faqs.answer: Yes, since it outputs standard RSS feeds, it works with any feed reader that supports RSS or Atom subscriptions.

faqs.question: Is Upvote RSS the same as Upvoty?
faqs.answer: No, they are unrelated products. Upvote RSS generates RSS feeds from social platforms, while Upvoty is a separate customer feedback and feature-voting SaaS tool.

use_cases.title: Curating a low-noise Reddit feed
use_cases.description: Users generate a filtered feed of only the highest-scoring posts from specific subreddits to avoid checking Reddit directly.

use_cases.title: Following Hacker News without the firehose
use_cases.description: Readers set score and keyword thresholds to receive only the most relevant Hacker News stories in their feed reader.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted content aggregation for personal dashboards
use_cases.description: Self-hosting enthusiasts combine Upvote RSS with tools like FreshRSS to build a personalized, ad-free content aggregation stack.

features: Optional AI summaries with your own API key

enrichment.short_description: Upvoty is an all-in-one customer feedback platform where product teams collect feature requests, let users vote on ideas, and communicate progress through public roadmaps and changelogs.
enrichment.seo_title: Upvoty Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Upvoty lets teams collect feedback, run public roadmaps, and ship changelogs. Compare its pricing plans and features before you sign up.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Upvoty is a bootstrapped customer feedback management SaaS founded in 2018 by Mike Strives and based in Eindhoven, Netherlands. It gives product and support teams a public or private feedback board where customers submit ideas, vote and comment on existing suggestions, and follow along as items move through a public roadmap from planned to in-progress to shipped. When a feature launches, Upvoty can automatically notify the users who requested it via an integrated changelog. Additional features include AI-assisted smart tagging to categorize incoming feedback, an AI-powered merge tool that detects and consolidates duplicate suggestions, single sign-on, custom domains and branding, multi-language support across 20-plus languages, and integrations with Slack, Jira, Intercom, Zapier, and a REST API, plus an MCP integration for querying feedback data from AI assistants like ChatGPT and Claude. As of a 2023 revenue disclosure, Upvoty reported around $480K in annual recurring revenue as a small, self-funded, bootstrapped team, and it says it is trusted by more than 2,000 product teams including companies like Veed. Pricing is subscription-based across four tiers, Power at $15/month, Super at $25/month (its most popular plan, adding changelogs and all integrations), Hyper at $49/month (unlimited projects and API/webhooks), and a custom-priced Enterprise tier with SAML SSO and a dedicated success manager, all backed by a 14-day free trial requiring no credit card. Upvoty competes with feedback and roadmap tools such as Canny, Featurebase, UserVoice, and Frill, generally positioning itself as a simpler, more affordable option for small and mid-sized product teams rather than large enterprises.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Upvoty
paragraphs: Upvoty is a customer feedback and feature-voting platform that helps product teams collect ideas, let users vote on what matters most, and keep everyone informed through public roadmaps and changelogs. Founded in 2018 by Mike Strives as a bootstrapped, self-funded company in Eindhoven, Netherlands, it targets startups and small-to-mid-sized product teams looking for a lightweight alternative to enterprise feedback tools.

Customers embed a feedback board on their website or app, where users submit and upvote suggestions. Product teams triage that input, move items onto a public roadmap, and automatically notify requesters via changelog entries once a feature ships.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core functionality includes public or private feedback boards with tagging, filtering, and moderation controls, a public roadmap view, and an automated changelog that notifies users when their requested features are released. AI-powered smart tags help categorize incoming feedback automatically, and an AI merge tool detects and consolidates duplicate suggestions to keep boards organized.

Upvoty also supports single sign-on, custom domains, white-label branding, more than 20 languages, and integrations with Slack, Jira, Intercom, Zapier, and its REST API, along with an MCP integration that lets AI assistants like ChatGPT and Claude query feedback data directly.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Upvoty offers four subscription tiers: Power at $15/month for a single project with unlimited boards and tracked users; Super at $25/month, its most popular plan, which adds changelogs, all integrations, and priority support; Hyper at $49/month, which unlocks unlimited projects, API and webhooks, and the Upvoty MCP integration; and a custom-priced Enterprise plan with unlimited everything, SAML SSO, and a dedicated success manager.
All plans include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required, and annual billing offers roughly a 10 percent discount versus paying monthly.

features.title: Feedback boards
features.description: Public or private boards where users submit, vote on, and comment on feature suggestions.

features.title: Public roadmap
features.description: Shows planned, in-progress, and shipped items to keep customers informed and build trust.

features.title: Automated changelog
features.description: Notifies requesters automatically when the features they voted for ship.

features.title: AI smart tags
features.description: Uses AI to automatically categorize and tag incoming feedback for easier triage.

features.title: AI duplicate merging
features.description: Detects and merges duplicate feedback items automatically to keep boards clean.

features.title: Custom branding and domains
features.description: White-label boards with custom domains and no Upvoty branding on paid plans.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Slack, Jira, Intercom, Zapier, and a REST API, plus MCP for AI assistants.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Feedback boards can be localized into more than 20 languages for global audiences.

pros: Affordable entry-level pricing compared to enterprise feedback tools
AI-assisted tagging and duplicate merging reduce manual triage work
Simple setup with public roadmap and changelog built in
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Wide language support for international products
cons: Lower tiers limit to a single project
Smaller ecosystem than larger competitors like Canny
Public pricing page omits some enterprise-only detail, requiring a sales contact
Small, bootstrapped team may mean slower feature velocity than VC-backed rivals

faqs.question: What does Upvoty do?
faqs.answer: Upvoty is a feedback management platform where product teams collect user suggestions, let customers vote on them, and communicate progress through public roadmaps and changelogs.

faqs.question: How much does Upvoty cost?
faqs.answer: Upvoty starts at $15/month for the Power plan, with Super at $25/month, Hyper at $49/month, and custom Enterprise pricing.

faqs.question: Does Upvoty offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Who founded Upvoty?
faqs.answer: Upvoty was founded in 2018 by Mike Strives and is based in Eindhoven, Netherlands.

faqs.question: Does Upvoty support single sign-on?
faqs.answer: Yes, custom SSO is available starting on the Power plan, with SAML SSO included in Enterprise.

faqs.question: Can Upvoty detect duplicate feedback?
faqs.answer: Yes, its AI-powered merge tool automatically detects and consolidates duplicate suggestions.

faqs.question: What integrations does Upvoty support?
faqs.answer: Upvoty integrates with Slack, Jira, Intercom, Zapier, its own API, and offers an MCP integration for AI assistants.

faqs.question: Is Upvoty a good fit for enterprise companies?
faqs.answer: Upvoty offers a custom Enterprise plan with SAML SSO, but it is primarily built and priced for startups and small-to-mid-sized product teams.

use_cases.title: Prioritizing the product roadmap
use_cases.description: Product managers use vote counts on the feedback board to decide which features to build next.

use_cases.title: Closing the feedback loop with customers
use_cases.description: Support and customer success teams notify users automatically via changelog when their requested features ship.

use_cases.title: Building a public-facing roadmap
use_cases.description: Startups embed a branded, public roadmap on their website to build transparency and trust with early customers.

enrichment.short_description: UrBackup is a free, open-source client-server backup system that combines fast incremental file backups with full image backups for Windows, Linux, and macOS.
enrichment.seo_title: UrBackup: Free Open-Source Client-Server Backup Software
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UrBackup is a free, open-source backup system combining image and file backups with fast restore. See its features, licensing, and how it compares.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: UrBackup is a free, open-source client-server backup solution created by developer Martin Raiber, with the project dating back to around 2011. It is licensed under the OSI-certified AGPLv3+, meaning it can be used, modified, and redistributed for free in both personal and commercial settings under open-source license terms. UrBackup combines full disk/image backups with continuous, incremental file backups: a lightweight client monitors folders for changes in real time so that new or modified files are backed up quickly without waiting for a scheduled full run, and backups can proceed even while files are open, such as active Outlook archives. The server exposes a web interface for configuring backup jobs, managing clients, and monitoring backup status, and offers multiple restore paths, restoring from the web UI, from the client application, directly through Windows Explorer, or via a bootable USB/CD for full bare-metal recovery of a crashed system. UrBackup also deduplicates files across clients so identical files stored by multiple machines are only kept once on the server, saving disk space, and it supports backing up remote or mobile clients over the internet, not just local networks. It runs on Windows, Linux, macOS, FreeBSD, and various NAS platforms, making it popular with home labs, small businesses, and IT administrators who want self-hosted backup infrastructure without per-device licensing fees. The project reports more than 21,000 active server instances with auto-update enabled. UrBackup is maintained by its original author alongside a company, Infscape UG, and continues active development on GitHub, with the free open-source edition as its only distribution model -- there is no separate paid commercial tier disclosed publicly.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is UrBackup
paragraphs: UrBackup is a free, open-source backup system built around a client-server architecture, created by developer Martin Raiber with the project active since roughly 2011. It combines full image backups with continuous incremental file backups so that both bare-metal recovery and quick file-level restores are possible from the same system.

The software is distributed under the AGPLv3+ open-source license, so anyone can install, use, and modify it, including for commercial purposes, without paying license fees. It runs across Windows, Linux, macOS, FreeBSD, and several NAS platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: UrBackup clients continuously monitor watched folders for changes, allowing fast incremental backups without waiting for a scheduled full scan, and can back up files that are actively open, such as email archives. The server provides a web interface for configuring jobs and checking client status, plus deduplication that stores identical files across multiple clients only once.

Restores can be performed through the web interface, the client app, directly via Windows Explorer, or from a bootable USB or CD for full bare-metal system recovery. UrBackup also supports backing up remote and mobile clients over the internet, not just devices on a local network.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: UrBackup is entirely free and open source under the AGPLv3+ license, with no paid tiers or per-device licensing costs disclosed publicly. Users are responsible for their own server and client hardware, and community support is available through the project's forums and GitHub issue tracker.

features.title: Deduplication
features.description: Stores identical files shared across multiple clients only once to reduce storage use.

features.title: Bare-metal recovery
features.description: Supports restoring a full system from a bootable USB or CD after a crash.

features.title: Remote and mobile client backup
features.description: Backs up devices over the internet, not just on the local network.

features.title: Cross-platform clients
features.description: Supports Windows, Linux, macOS, FreeBSD, and various NAS operating systems.

pros: Completely free and open source with no per-device fees
Combines image and file-level backup in one system
Handles open files without interrupting active use
Deduplication reduces storage costs across many clients
Supports remote/internet backups, not just LAN
cons: Interface is more utilitarian than modern commercial backup dashboards
No official vendor support contract; relies on community and forums
Initial setup and tuning require some technical/sysadmin skill
Smaller ecosystem of integrations compared to commercial backup suites

faqs.question: Is UrBackup really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, UrBackup is free, open-source software licensed under AGPLv3+, usable for both personal and commercial purposes.

faqs.question: Who created UrBackup?
faqs.answer: UrBackup was created by developer Martin Raiber, with the project active since around 2011.

faqs.question: Does UrBackup support bare-metal recovery?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can restore a full system from a bootable USB or CD after hardware failure or a crash.

faqs.question: What operating systems does UrBackup support?
faqs.answer: It supports Windows, Linux, macOS, FreeBSD, and several NAS platforms for both server and client.

faqs.question: Can UrBackup back up files that are currently open?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can back up open files such as active Outlook archives without interrupting the running application.

faqs.question: Does UrBackup deduplicate data?
faqs.answer: Yes, identical files stored by multiple clients are only stored once on the server to save space.

faqs.question: Can UrBackup back up remote devices over the internet?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports backing up remote and mobile clients over the internet in addition to local network clients.

faqs.question: How many UrBackup servers are in use?
faqs.answer: The project reports more than 21,000 active server instances with auto-update enabled.

use_cases.title: Home lab and self-hosted backup

use_cases.title: Disaster recovery for critical systems
use_cases.description: Organizations use UrBackup's image backups and bootable recovery media to restore crashed servers quickly after hardware failure.

enrichment.short_description: UserGems is an AI-powered sales intelligence platform that tracks buyer job changes and 30-plus other intent signals to help B2B sales and marketing teams find and reach the right prospects.
enrichment.seo_title: UserGems Review: Sales Intelligence and Job-Change Tracking
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UserGems tracks buyer job changes and intent signals for B2B sales teams. See how it works, its AI scoring, and what it costs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: UserGems is a B2B sales intelligence and account-based marketing platform founded in 2019 by Christian Kletzl, headquartered in San Francisco, California. The company has raised roughly $22.5 million from investors including Battery Ventures, Tiger Global Management, and Kearny Jackson. UserGems was originally best known for tracking job changes among past customers and champions, alerting sales reps when a former buyer moves to a new company, since that buyer is statistically more likely to purchase again. The platform has since expanded into a broader AI-driven outbound and ABM command center: it aggregates more than 30 native intent signals, including job changes, website visits, hiring activity, and M&A events, and combines them with data pulled from CRMs, call transcripts, and product usage. A proprietary AI system called Gem-E analyzes hundreds of signals to build transparent, customizable lead-scoring models that learn from each customer's own closed-won deal patterns, and the platform can deploy AI agents to orchestrate personalized, multi-channel outreach across ads, email, and CRM updates, targeting entire buying groups rather than single contacts. UserGems integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, Outreach, Salesloft, LinkedIn, Meta, and Google Ads, and is SOC 2 certified with GDPR and CCPA compliance. Pricing is not published; prospective customers must request a demo, and the company offers a guarantee of at least 1x revenue ROI or a full refund, citing customer results such as a median 47x pipeline return. UserGems competes with sales intelligence and intent-data platforms like ZoomInfo, 6sense, Clearbit (now part of HubSpot), and Apollo.io, and is generally positioned toward mid-market and enterprise B2B sales teams running outbound and ABM programs rather than small businesses.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is UserGems
paragraphs: UserGems is a B2B sales intelligence platform, founded in 2019 by Christian Kletzl and based in San Francisco, that helps sales and marketing teams identify which buyers to target and when. It started with a focused idea: tracking job changes among a company's past customers and champions, since a former buyer moving to a new company is one of the strongest predictors of a new sale.

The platform has grown into a wider AI-driven command center for outbound and account-based marketing, combining more than 30 buying signals with an AI scoring and outreach engine to help revenue teams prioritize and reach the right accounts at the right time.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: UserGems aggregates signals such as job changes, hiring activity, website visits, and M&A events, and layers in data from a company's own CRM, call transcripts, and product usage. Its proprietary AI system, Gem-E, scores accounts and contacts using hundreds of signals and builds custom scoring models that learn from each customer's historical closed-won patterns.

On the activation side, UserGems can orchestrate outreach across ads, email sequences, and CRM record updates, coordinating messaging to multiple stakeholders within a target buying group simultaneously, and syncs with tools like Salesforce, HubSpot, Outreach, and Salesloft.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: UserGems does not publish pricing on its website; prospective customers must request a demo and receive a custom quote based on team size and desired signals/features. The company backs its platform with a guarantee of at least 1x revenue ROI or a full refund, and it markets outcomes such as a median 47x pipeline ROI from customer case studies.

Combines a wide range of intent signals rather than a single data point
ROI guarantee reduces risk for prospective customers

AI scoring adapts to each customer's own historical deal data
cons: No public pricing, requiring a sales conversation to get a quote
Likely priced for mid-market and enterprise budgets rather than small teams
Effectiveness depends heavily on the quality of connected CRM data
Newer AI-agent outreach features are less established than the core job-change tracking product

faqs.question: What does UserGems do?
faqs.answer: UserGems is a B2B sales intelligence platform that tracks buyer job changes and other intent signals, then uses AI to score and prioritize accounts for outbound sales and marketing.

faqs.question: When was UserGems founded?
faqs.answer: UserGems was founded in 2019 by Christian Kletzl and is based in San Francisco, California.

faqs.question: How much does UserGems cost?
faqs.answer: UserGems does not publish pricing; you need to request a demo and receive a custom quote.

faqs.question: What is Gem-E?
faqs.answer: Gem-E is UserGems' proprietary AI agent that analyzes hundreds of signals to build transparent, customizable account and contact scoring models.

faqs.question: What CRMs does UserGems integrate with?
faqs.answer: UserGems integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, Outreach, and Salesloft, among other sales and marketing tools.

faqs.question: Does UserGems offer a guarantee?
faqs.answer: Yes, UserGems offers a minimum 1x revenue ROI guarantee or a full refund.

faqs.question: How much funding has UserGems raised?
faqs.answer: UserGems has raised approximately $22.5 million from investors including Battery Ventures and Tiger Global Management.

faqs.question: Is UserGems only for job-change tracking?
faqs.answer: No, while job-change tracking was its original focus, UserGems now aggregates 30-plus signals and offers AI-driven outreach orchestration across multiple channels.

enrichment.short_description: UserGuiding is a no-code product adoption platform for building in-app onboarding tours, tooltips, checklists, and self-service support content without developer help.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UserGuiding is a no-code onboarding and product adoption tool. Compare its pricing plans, features, and how it stacks up against alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: UserGuiding is a no-code product adoption and user onboarding platform founded in 2017 and headquartered in Istanbul, Turkey, with additional presence in the United States. The company has raised a relatively modest $1.1 million in funding from investors including Angel Effect and Collective Spark, and reported around $3.3 million in annual revenue with roughly 130 customers as of 2024, reflecting a bootstrapped-leaning, capital-efficient growth path. UserGuiding lets product and customer success teams build in-app experiences, including guided product tours, tooltips, hotspots, banners, modals, and checklists, using a no-code Chrome extension rather than requiring engineering resources, with a stated setup time of around 15 minutes. Beyond onboarding, the platform provides self-service support tools such as an in-app knowledge base, an AI Assistant, and Resource Centers, plus feedback and analytics features like in-app surveys, NPS tracking, session replay, and behavioral segmentation. An AI Assistant with MCP Server integration lets the tool connect to external AI systems. UserGuiding serves more than 1,000 teams, including reported customers like Uber, Elastic, Renault, Total Energies, and Digital Ocean, and holds SOC 2 Type 2, GDPR, HIPAA, ISO 27001, and LGPD compliance certifications. Pricing follows a freemium model: a free Support Essentials plan covers the knowledge base and resource center; the Starter plan costs $174/month billed annually (with a higher month-to-month rate) for up to 100 active guides, 5,000 session recordings, and 5 team seats; the Growth plan is $349/month billed annually and adds A/B testing, custom CSS, localization, and premium integrations; and a custom-priced Enterprise plan adds compliance certifications, SAML SSO, and dedicated onboarding support. UserGuiding is most often compared to Userpilot, Appcues, Chameleon, and WalkMe, and is generally positioned as a simpler, faster-to-implement, and lower-priced alternative to those competitors for small and mid-sized SaaS companies.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is UserGuiding
paragraphs: UserGuiding is a no-code product adoption platform, founded in 2017 and based in Istanbul, Turkey, that lets product and customer success teams build in-app onboarding experiences without writing code. Using a Chrome extension, teams can create guided tours, tooltips, and checklists and publish them in minutes rather than waiting on engineering resources.

Beyond onboarding, UserGuiding also offers self-service support tools, including an in-app knowledge base and AI Assistant, as well as feedback and analytics features like NPS surveys and session replay, positioning it as an all-in-one product adoption suite rather than a single-purpose tour builder.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core building blocks include product tours, tooltips, hotspots, banners, modals, and checklists, all created through a no-code Chrome extension with instant preview and publishing. In-app support tools include a searchable knowledge base, an AI Assistant with MCP Server integration, and Resource Centers for self-service help.

The platform also supports in-app surveys and NPS tracking, session replay, and behavioral segmentation for personalizing which experiences different user groups see, plus more than 15 total tools across onboarding, engagement, and analytics categories.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: UserGuiding offers a free Support Essentials plan covering the knowledge base, resource center, and AI Assistant with unlimited domains. The paid Starter plan costs $174/month billed annually and includes up to 100 active guides, unlimited hotspots and checklists, 10 active surveys, 5,000 session recordings, and 5 team seats.

The Growth plan is $349/month billed annually and adds A/B testing, goal tracking, custom CSS, localization, premium integrations, and 15 team seats. An Enterprise plan offers custom pricing with SOC 2, GDPR, and HIPAA compliance, SAML SSO, and a dedicated customer success manager; all paid plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee.

Broad feature set spanning onboarding, support, and analytics in one tool
Strong compliance certifications (SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA, ISO 27001)

30-day money-back guarantee on paid plans
cons: Session recording and guide limits on lower tiers may be restrictive for larger products
Advanced features like A/B testing require the higher Growth tier
Smaller funding and team size than larger competitors like WalkMe
Chrome extension-based editor may feel less flexible than fully code-based customization

faqs.question: What is UserGuiding used for?
faqs.answer: UserGuiding is used to build no-code in-app onboarding experiences, product tours, and self-service support content to improve user adoption.

faqs.question: Does UserGuiding have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Support Essentials plan is free forever and includes the knowledge base and resource center features.

faqs.question: How much does UserGuiding cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $174/month for Starter billed annually, with Growth at $349/month annually and custom Enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: When was UserGuiding founded?
faqs.answer: UserGuiding was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Istanbul, Turkey.

faqs.question: Does UserGuiding require coding skills?

faqs.question: Is UserGuiding SOC 2 compliant?

faqs.question: Can UserGuiding record user sessions?
faqs.answer: Yes, session replay is included starting on the Starter plan, with a set number of recordings per month.

faqs.question: What companies use UserGuiding?
faqs.answer: UserGuiding says it is used by more than 1,000 teams, including reported customers like Uber, Elastic, Renault, and Digital Ocean.

enrichment.short_description: Userlist is a customer messaging and lifecycle email automation platform built specifically for SaaS companies, combining visual workflows, in-app messages, and account-level segmentation.
enrichment.seo_title: Userlist Review: SaaS Email Automation Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Userlist is a lifecycle email and in-app messaging tool built for SaaS. See its pricing, key features, and how it compares to Customer.io and Intercom.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Userlist is a customer messaging and lifecycle email automation platform purpose-built for SaaS companies, founded in 2017 by Jane Portman, Benedikt Deicke, and Claire Suellentrop and based in Atlanta, Georgia. The founders signed an informal collaboration agreement in October 2017 and the company later joined the 2020 batch of TinySeed, the SaaS-focused accelerator run by Rob Walling and Einar Vollset, remaining a small, bootstrapped-style team of around five people as of recent public revenue data showing about $1 million in annual revenue. Userlist lets product marketers and founders build customer lifecycle campaigns through a visual workflow builder, combining marketing email, transactional email (receipts, password resets, notifications), and in-app messages and broadcasts in one tool. Its key differentiator is native support for account-level, B2B-style data structures: unlike many email tools built for one-to-one consumer relationships, Userlist models many-to-many relationships between users and companies with different roles, letting teams automate messaging based on account-level behavior rather than only individual user actions. Other features include dynamic segmentation, A/B/C testing with conversion goal tracking, custom properties and events, webhook and API access, and subscription preference management. Userlist positions itself directly against Intercom, Drip, and Customer.io, arguing that generic tools require workarounds for SaaS-specific account structures that Userlist handles natively, and it emphasizes direct support from its own engineers rather than a large support organization. Pricing is usage-based on monthly active users: the Basic plan starts around $119-149/month for up to 10,000 MAU with additional users billed per 1,000, and includes lifecycle email, in-app messaging, and the workflow builder; the Professional plan costs more per MAU but adds A/B/C testing, advanced integrations, and unlimited custom events; and a custom Enterprise plan adds dedicated account management and done-for-you data planning. Annual billing offers roughly a 20 percent discount versus monthly.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Userlist
paragraphs: Userlist is a customer messaging and lifecycle email automation platform built specifically for SaaS companies, founded in 2017 by Jane Portman, Benedikt Deicke, and Claire Suellentrop and based in Atlanta, Georgia. The company joined the TinySeed SaaS accelerator's 2020 batch and has grown as a small, focused team rather than pursuing large-scale venture funding.

Unlike general-purpose email marketing tools, Userlist is designed around how SaaS products actually work: users often belong to companies, hold different roles, and their behavior needs to be tracked and automated at both the individual and account level.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Userlist's visual workflow builder automates customer journeys such as onboarding sequences, trial-conversion nudges, and re-engagement campaigns, combining marketing email, transactional email, and in-app messages in a single tool. It supports dynamic segmentation of both individual users and companies, letting teams target messages based on account-level behavior, not just individual actions.

Additional capabilities include A/B/C testing with conversion goal tracking, custom properties and events for detailed targeting, broadcast messaging, webhook integration, and an API for custom workflows, along with subscription preference management to keep unsubscribe handling compliant.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Userlist's Basic plan starts at roughly $119-149/month for up to 10,000 monthly active users, with additional users billed at about $10 per extra 1,000, and includes lifecycle and transactional email, in-app messaging, segmentation, and the visual workflow builder with 5 team seats. The Professional plan raises the per-MAU rate slightly (around $12 per 1,000 additional users) and adds A/B/C testing, advanced workflow features, more integrations, and unlimited custom events with 10 team seats.

A custom-priced Enterprise plan adds dedicated account management, done-for-you data migration and planning, and monthly review calls. Annual billing saves roughly 20 percent compared to paying monthly.

features.description: Natively models many-to-many relationships between users and companies for B2B-style targeting.

features.title: A/B/C testing
features.description: Tests multiple message variants against each other with conversion goal tracking.

features.title: Custom properties and events
features.description: Tracks custom user and company attributes and events to power precise targeting.

features.title: Webhooks and API
features.description: Connects Userlist to other tools and custom workflows via webhooks and a developer API.

pros: Purpose-built for SaaS account structures, unlike generic email tools
Combines lifecycle email, transactional email, and in-app messaging in one product
Small, hands-on support team of engineers rather than a large call center
Handles complex many-to-many user-to-company relationships natively
20 percent discount for annual billing
cons: Pricing scales with monthly active users, which can get costly at high volume
Smaller integration marketplace than larger platforms like Intercom
No published pricing for Professional or Enterprise tiers without contacting sales
Small team size may mean slower feature rollout compared to larger competitors

faqs.question: What is Userlist used for?
faqs.answer: Userlist is used by SaaS companies to automate lifecycle email, transactional email, and in-app messaging based on user and account behavior.

faqs.question: How much does Userlist cost?
faqs.answer: The Basic plan starts around $119-149/month for up to 10,000 monthly active users, with the Professional plan priced higher per MAU and Enterprise custom-priced.

faqs.question: Who founded Userlist?
faqs.answer: Userlist was founded in 2017 by Jane Portman, Benedikt Deicke, and Claire Suellentrop and is based in Atlanta, Georgia.

faqs.question: How is Userlist different from Intercom or Customer.io?
faqs.answer: Userlist natively supports account-level, many-to-many user-to-company data structures common in B2B SaaS, which generic tools like Intercom or Customer.io typically require workarounds to handle.

faqs.question: Does Userlist support transactional email?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can send transactional emails like receipts and password resets alongside marketing and lifecycle campaigns.

faqs.question: Does Userlist offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Userlist offers a 14-day free trial before requiring a paid subscription.

faqs.question: Is Userlist part of an accelerator program?
faqs.answer: Yes, Userlist joined the 2020 batch of TinySeed, a SaaS-focused startup accelerator.

faqs.question: Can Userlist run A/B tests?
faqs.answer: Yes, A/B/C testing with conversion goal tracking is available starting on the Professional plan.
